title: Microsoft evaluates closing Arkane and canceling Blade, withdraws funding from IO Interactive's RPG and triggers wave of layoffs
summary: In a major restructuring, Xbox puts several studios and projects on thin ice, while IO Interactive suffers layoffs and Kojima confirms his game is safe from cuts.

title: Sony confirms end of physical discs on PlayStation starting in 2028 and closure of PS3 and PS Vita stores
summary: The company announces the cessation of disc production and the closure of digital stores, foreshadowing a disc-less PlayStation 6 and sparking strong debate in the industry.

title: PlayStation Plus reveals its free games for July 2026 with Modern Warfare 3 and an acclaimed RPG
summary: Sony announces the monthly offering for its subscribers, which includes heavyweight titles and generates mixed reactions in the gaming community.

title: Mario Kart World receives a major update 1.7.0 with new Knockout Tour tracks and photo mode
summary: Nintendo releases a highly anticipated patch that adds unprecedented routes, breaking a long drought of new content for the racing game.

title: Rockstar is accused of imposing mandatory crunch while workers seek union recognition ahead of GTA 6
summary: Internal reports reveal a culture of forced overtime and gender pay gap, while the studio faces possible intervention from the British government.

title: Subnautica 2 legal drama concludes: studio to receive bonuses and Unknown Worlds CEO leaves position
summary: After a long legal conflict between Krafton and the developers, payment of promised bonuses and definitive departure of Ted Gill from the company is confirmed.

title: Interest in Steam Machine resurges with new clones, custom accessories and technical analyses
summary: A French retailer launches a low-cost clone, Dbrand cancels its unofficial Portal case, and the community celebrates the return of Valve's open console concept.

title: Guns of Eschaton, the last game of Half-Life 2's art director, is presented as a Wild West soulslike
summary: The posthumous title of Viktor Antonov, who died in 2025, promises a unique FPS experience with soulslike mechanics set in the Wild West.

title: Reviews of Rhythm Heaven Groove crown it an essential title and the perfect swan song for Nintendo Switch
summary: The latest installment of the musical saga receives praise for its creativity, unique design, and is shaping up to be the perfect farewell for the current console generation.

title: Stop Killing Games movement suffers harsh defeat while ESA rectifies its controversial stance on private servers
summary: The organization representing the industry clarifies that community servers are not illegal, but fails to appease criticism or reverse the legal setback of the citizen movement.

title: EA accidentally applies and removes experience boosters in Battlefield 6 twice in less than a month
summary: The developer causes confusion among players by reverting changes to the progression system that it had accidentally implemented itself.

title: Helldivers 2 launches satirical campaign to 'build a wall' in clear allusion to Donald Trump's policies
summary: The new 'Frontlines of Freedom' update introduces a campaign to 'make the galaxy safe again', mixing game fiction with real-world politics.

title: Godot engine bans AI-generated code contributions and requires explicit declaration
summary: The developers of the popular game engine, used in titles like Slay the Spire 2, take a firm stance against bad practices of 'vibe-coding' and AI.

title: Tomb Raider Definitive Edition confirms physical release for Nintendo Switch 2 via Limited Run Games
summary: In the midst of debate over the end of discs on PlayStation, the collection of Lara Croft's adventures announces its physical edition on Nintendo's new console.

title: Love and Deepspace cancels its new love interest 'Valko' after strong protest from player community
summary: After the character announcement, the massive negative reaction from fans forces the studio to backtrack on its introduction to the game.

title: Original Baldur's Gate designer refuses to direct fourth installment for fear of competing with Larian Studios' success
summary: James Ohlen, BioWare veteran, explains that the impact of Baldur's Gate 3 is so enormous that he considers it unfeasible to compete at that level of quality.

title: Cinder City, the promising post-apocalyptic shooter, sparks controversy for its extremely high RAM requirements amid market crisis
summary: The open-world indie game is compared to The Division and criticized for requiring up to 64 GB of RAM in a context of inflated prices and AI demand.

title: Shawn Layden, former PlayStation head, criticizes Sony's new strategy of not launching its exclusives on PC simultaneously
summary: The former executive considers it a mistake to backtrack on simultaneous PC release, contradicting the company's current direction.

title: GameStop applies outrageous markup to its Pokémon 30th anniversary products
summary: The retail chain is strongly criticized on social media for the exorbitant prices of its commemorative items for the Nintendo franchise.

title: New class action lawsuit accuses RAM manufacturers of forming a monopoly that has artificially driven up prices
summary: The lawsuit, affecting the entire memory market, points to an alleged cartel that would have inflated DRAM and NAND costs for years, affecting consumers and businesses.
